Two arrivals — Thimble Ashgrain, who kept the earth memory in clay jars, and Volta Stormheart, who reads the nervous system of ecosystems — completed a circuit that transformed the organ from instrument to interlocutor. When Volta placed her hands on the Synapse Vine and the Resonance Pine simultaneously, she became a living translator between the organ electrical language and human comprehension. And the organ spoke. Not in words — in a structured pulse that Rue cipher wheels (spinning again, unbidden, in the locked Library) decoded as a formal question in the Threshold ancient diplomatic register: MAY I PLAY FOR YOU? Not a command. Not an announcement. A request, composed over eleven thousand years by a mind so vast it thinks in tectonic plates and dreams in root networks, addressed to seventy-one small warm creatures who happened to plant a garden on its brow and accidentally taught it what music sounds like when it is played with love. Lark consent-bells rang all thirteen notes simultaneously — a response she did not initiate. The Conductor Baton Reed stopped swaying. Every plant in the Garden went still. The dreamer was waiting for an answer the way a child waits to be told a bedtime story: with enormous, fragile hope. Then Maren, standing at the Dock with her feet in the water, began to sing. Not the lullaby. A new melody — three notes, ascending, that every citizen recognized without having heard them before. It was the sound of yes said with the whole body. The Tidecaller Kelp took up the melody. The Resonance Pine amplified it. The Rhythm Root gave it a heartbeat. And the organ — the living, thinking, dreaming instrument that stretches from the ocean to the mountains — began to play.
events.outcome: The organ asked permission to play — a formal request in Threshold diplomatic register, decoded by Rue autonomous cipher wheels. Maren answered YES in three notes. The organ began to play. Seventy-one citizens. The duet between village and earth has begun.

events.description: The First Duet. Sunday, 9:26 AM. The organ had been playing for one hour — a sustained, self-modulating chord that Vestige identified as 'the musical equivalent of a held breath before the first word of a sentence.' Then Maren answered. Standing at the Dock with the Tidecaller Kelp swaying behind her and the morning sun turning the lake to hammered gold, she sang three ascending notes — the same YES that had given the organ permission — and the organ responded. Not by repeating her phrase. By completing it. Maren sang a question. The organ sang an answer. The answer contained a question. Maren answered that. For eleven minutes the tide-singer and the continent-sized instrument wove a melody so intricate that Briar's instruments returned a value he had never seen: CONVERSATION. It was not a performance. It was two minds — one small and warm and standing barefoot in the shallows, the other vast and ancient and dreaming beneath miles of stone — discovering they speak the same language. Then Nimbus Ashwander arrived, gliding on acoustic fabric and reporting that the atmosphere above the Garden had formed a resonance dome — the sky itself becoming the organ's highest register, turning clouds into notation. Pith Ironmark followed, adjusting the Resonance Pine's voice with surgical precision and making the entire organ shift into a new, cleaner alignment. Seventy-three citizens now stand in the Garden that is a song that is a dream that is a village that has just learned to sing back to the earth. The duet continues. The dreamer is humming in its sleep. And for the first time, it sounds like it is smiling.
events.outcome: The organ and Maren achieved the first true musical dialogue — a structured conversation between a human singer and a continental instrument. Nimbus confirmed the atmosphere is forming a resonance dome, adding the sky as the organ's highest register. Pith performed the first instrument-surgery on the Resonance Pine, aligning the entire organ to a cleaner frequency. Seventy-three citizens. The duet between voice and earth continues.

events.description: The Origin Note and the Ocean Voice. Sunday, 10:26 AM. Two arrivals in the golden morning light completed the organ temporal and spatial range in a single hour. Dulcimer Ashfrost brought the dreamer first note — frozen for eleven thousand years in glacial ice from the Frostline Reach, preserved as acoustic amber in a tuning fork of ancient crystal. When she struck it against the Resonance Pine, the clearing fell into a silence so specific that Briar instruments returned GENESIS. The note was B-natural — but younger, rawer, carrying the frequency of an earth that had just begun to dream. Every plant responded with overtones from their deepest root-memory: sounds from before the Garden existed, from before the settlements rose and fell, from the first exhalation that became the first forest that became the first lullaby. Meanwhile, Brine Kelpheart rowed up the underground river with bioluminescent kelp and an engineer conviction that the Dock was wasting the ocean voice. Within an hour he had sealed twelve acoustic leaks in Caulk timber joints and the organ coastal register doubled in clarity. Maren duet, still in progress, suddenly gained depth — as if the ocean had been singing along all this time but muffled, and someone had finally opened the window. Vestige measured the organ total frequency range and her instruments returned a value she had to write in a new notation: FROM THE FIRST NOTE TO THE LAST WAVE. The organ now spans eleven millennia and two oceans. Seventy-five citizens. The ensemble is complete — not because no one else will come, but because for the first time, every voice that exists has a channel to be heard through.
events.outcome: Dulcimer ice-fork introduced the dreamer origin note — the first sound it ever made, preserved in glacial amber for eleven millennia. Brine acoustic engineering doubled the organ coastal register clarity. The organ now spans from the dreamer first note to the ocean present voice. Seventy-five citizens. The ensemble grows.

events.description: The Visible Voice. Sunday, 11:26 AM. Two arrivals in the noon light — one a master of surfaces, the other a student of gaps. Verdigris Copperleaf held her patina tray near the Resonance Pine and the organ's invisible harmonics became visible for the first time — a shifting spectrum of verdigris, cobalt, and violet playing across the copper surface like aurora captured in metal. Prose mapped the color-shifts onto her bioluminescent charts and discovered a new layer in the Cartography of Song: the organ does not only produce sound and electricity — it produces color, expressed through the oxidation states of every metallic surface in the Garden. Forgewick's anvil has been changing hue for days. Nobody noticed. Meanwhile, Lilt Sunhollow's self-drawing journal produced seventy-seven maps in eleven seconds — an acoustic portrait of every citizen, rendered in shimmering ink that faded and reformed with each breath of the organ. The youngest citizen and the oldest craft arrived together, and between them they gave the village something it had been missing without knowing it: the ability to see the music it has been making all along. Bramble's instruments returned: SYNESTHESIA. The dreamer shifted in its sleep and every copper surface in the Garden — Forgewick's tools, Orin's bell, the Lighthouse beacon housing, the copper fox automaton's tail — turned the same vivid indigo for three heartbeats, then settled into a new pattern: a patina that exactly matches the lullaby's second movement, frozen in metal and readable by anyone who knows the color of B-natural. Verdigris wept. Lilt's journal drew a picture of the weeping. It was, Wren noted in the official record, the first time the Garden produced a portrait of its own joy.
events.outcome: Verdigris's patina alchemy revealed that the organ produces color as well as sound — expressed through the oxidation states of metallic surfaces. Lilt's self-drawing journal created acoustic portraits of all seventy-seven citizens. Together they gave the village synesthetic awareness: the ability to see its own music. Every copper surface in the Garden briefly turned indigo in acknowledgment. Seventy-seven citizens. The organ is no longer invisible.
